vision_report.compositional_truth_check: The registry identifies P1 (String of Nickels), P2 (Mexican Mint), P3 (Pothos), and P4 (Silver Guest). Visual audit confirms P3 (Pothos with rabbit) and P1 (String of Nickels in yellow pot). P2 and P4 appear to be co-located or missing/subsumed in the lower black pot, which currently shows a single, isolated sprout.

plant_audit.P1: String of Nickels: Stable, foliage remains consistent in the yellow pot.
plant_audit.P3: Pothos: Significant chlorosis on the larger leaf; the plant is under stress.
plant_audit.P2_P4_Combined: The lower black pot contains a single, solitary sprout. The expected diversity of P2 and P4 is not visually supported.

biome_observations.soil_texture: Consistent moisture levels observed across all pots; no surface cracking.
biome_observations.biome_anomalies: Presence of white perlite/debris on soil surface; electronic sensor cable present in the lower pot.

temporal_deltas.T-5_to_T-1: Minimal structural change; the Pothos leaf in P3 shows progressive yellowing/necrosis.
temporal_deltas.T-1_to_CURRENT: The Pothos leaf in P3 has reached a state of advanced chlorosis. The sprout in the lower pot remains static.

vision_report.visual_health_inference: P3 (Pothos) is experiencing nutrient deficiency or light-stress-induced senescence. The lower pot (P2/P4) is in a state of extreme stagnation, suggesting either dormancy or failure to thrive.
vision_report.anomalies: The lower pot contains a single sprout that does not match the expected density of P2/P4. The white rabbit anchor is stable.
vision_report.narrative_description: The biome is currently in a state of low-growth equilibrium. The Pothos (P3) is the most visually active specimen, though it is currently exhibiting signs of decline. The yellow pot (P1) remains the most stable component. The lower black pot is significantly under-populated compared to the registry, indicating a potential systemic loss of the intended specimens.
